summary: Sparkling soundboard recording of an all‑out Europe ’72 show featuring a monster Dark Star and standout Looks Like Rain with pedal steel.
review: The performance is hailed as one of the band’s finest – every song brims with energy, highlighted by a soaring Dark Star, a gorgeous pedal‑steel rendition of Looks Like Rain and a tight Good Lovin’/Caution sandwich. Charlie Miller’s meticulous SBD mix delivers crystal‑clear, balanced sound, though a few listeners note weak vocals on Goin’ Down the Road and a sub‑par Cumberland Blues. Overall the show is an exhilarating, near‑perfect snapshot of the band at their 1972 peak.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive reviews (15 +), most giving five stars and praising both musicianship and Miller’s soundboard mix; only minor, isolated complaints about two songs.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Sparkling, crisp SBD mix with excellent instrument separation (Keith/organ front‑center, pedal steel highlighted). Miller’s patch work fixes drop‑outs; occasional vocal imperfections on GDTRFB and a weak Cumberland Blues track noted.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Two 15‑song sets packed with climactic peaks – Dark Star into Sugar Magnolia, Good Lovin’/Kitchen Sink/Lovin’ Reprise, and a NFA‑GDTRFB‑NFA trio – delivering nonstop energy and seamless transitions.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Pedal steel on Looks Like Rain praised; his guitar work in Dark Star described as telepathic and flawless.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned as part of the band’s telepathic link, no specific solo comments.
band_member_comments.Bob: Contributed to the high‑energy ensemble feel; no individual remarks.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith and Pipgen's organ/keyboard blend highlighted as a key texture, sitting nicely in the mix.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann praised for his freedom as the sole drummer on this show.
